title: Nomo
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|the traffic controller|the [[Clawdite/Legends|Clawdite]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Nomo Sliken]]}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image= |name=Nomo |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|By all the gods, look at that! We blew that ship out of the air!"
"''That'' was storage tank sixteen, you idiot.|Nomo and Zyak|}} [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] '''Nomo''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] who served in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Starfleet]] as an air-traffic controller during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Just after his graduation, he was posted to [[Fuel City]], a sizable refueling station on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]], during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. One [[Standard day/Legends|day]], Nomo was to serve as the shift replacement for [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zyak]], who otherwise ran Fuel City's traffic. However, when Nomo arrived, [[Mission to Sulon|fighting]] broke out around the docked [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Sulon Star]]''. Nomo and Zyak tried to control the situation, but after [[Storage tank thirteen|several]] [[Storage tank fourteen|fuel]] [[Storage tank fifteen|storage]] [[Storage tank sixteen|tanks]] were destroyed by the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]'', Zyak deserted and left Nomo in charge, much to the lieutenant's distress. ==Biography== {{Quote|Well, Nomo, it's ''your'' shift. Sort this one out and you'll be a Captain by Monday. Fail, and you'll be working in the mines."
"Mines? What mines? Where will you go?"
"As far as I can. As far as I can.|Captain Zyak and Lieutenant Nomo as Fuel City's fuel cells are destroyed|}} [[File:SulonStar.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Nomo worked in Fuel City, which briefly hosted the ship ''Sulon Star''.]] A young [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Nomo graduated from [[air-traffic control school]] during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'', as well as the [[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|audio drama]], to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. and with the help of several connections in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] Nomo secured a post as a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] in [[Fuel City]], a fueling depot located on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] whose traffic was controlled by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zyak]]. One [[Standard day/Legends|day]], Nomo was assigned to work at the end of Zyak's shift. When Nomo arrived, Zyak was just preparing to leave when a [[Lightfight|firefight]] [[Mission to Sulon|broke out]] inside and around the docked [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Sulon Star]]''. Feeling that Nomo could use some experience, Zyak decided to stay and help Nomo sort out the situation. Firstly, Zyak ordered Nomo to contact the individual in charge of the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] around the ''Sulon Star'' and tell them to cease fire, as they were in danger of disrupting any one of the fuel tanks in [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] [[Fuel City|station six]]. The stormtroopers' officer told Nomo that the infiltrator, a fledgling [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] named [[Kyle Katarn]], was to be [[Death/Legends|killed]] at any cost. Zyak dismissed the trooper officer and ordered Nomo to contact operations and stop the fuel pumps and bleed the pipes. A [[Unidentified air-traffic control technician|technician]] informed Zyak of an incoming ship, the ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]''. Zyak had encountered the ship earlier in the day, and had let the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], [[Jan Ors]], through. Realizing that Ors had dropped Katarn off in the station, and as such was responsible for the battle that was being fought, Zyak order Ors to be shot down. Ors avoided the missile response, and fired on [[storage tank sixteen]]. The ''Crow'' was lost in the explosion, and Nomo initially believed that the ship had been successfully destroyed. Zyak pointed out that wasn't the case, and Nomo reported that the fuel pipes had been emptied seventy-percent of the way. However, a relay went down, causing a delay in operations being able to work on the fuel valves. Moments later, [[Storage tank thirteen|storage tanks thirteen]], [[Storage tank fourteen|fourteen]] and [[Storage tank fifteen|fifteen]] blew one after the other, due to the fuel not having been completely emptied in time, which allowed the fuel in the pipes to act as fuses. Zyak, not wanting the blame to fall squarely on his shoulders, told Nomo that since it was his shift, he had to fix the issue. Zyak sarcastically added that if Nomo could sort the situation out, he'd become the next Captain by Monday, or else he'd be sent to work in [[Mining/Legends|mines]]. When Nomo noticed Zyak leaving, the captain informed Nomo that he was going AWOL. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Have they bled the pipes yet?"
"They're working on it—some kind of relay went down. What's so important about bleeding the...|Zyak and Nomo, shortly before several fuel tanks exploded|}} Nomo was a young, sallow-faced Human who had used his connections within the Empire to secure a position that he wanted. Zyak was aware of this, and brought it to Nomo's attention as a thinly-veiled insult. Nomo dutifully followed Zyak's orders, but was not able to tell that a storage tank had exploded, as opposed to a ship. Zyak referred to Nomo as an idiot because of that. In addition, Nomo did not know fuel station protocol. When Zyak began to leave, Nomo became visibly distressed as his face paled. ==Behind the scenes== Lieutenant Nomo was created by author [[William C. Dietz]] for the [[1998]] novella ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''. Nomo later appeared in the full cast [[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|audio drama that adapted the book]], but his voice actor was not credited. ==Appearances== *''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' {{1st}} *[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy officers of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]]
